quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Constraint optimization"?

options.text: To find absolute extrema on [a,b]: 1) find critical points, 2) evaluate f at critical points and endpoints, 3) compare values
d/dx[f(x) ± g(x)] = f'(x) ± g'(x); derivative of a sum/difference is the sum/difference of derivatives
Use a constraint equation to express the objective function in terms of one variable before finding critical points
If f'(x) = 0 for all x in an interval, MVT proves f must be a constant function on that interval

quiz_questions.explanation: "Constraint optimization" is defined as: Use a constraint equation to express the objective function in terms of one variable before finding critical points. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Feasible domain"?

options.text: F(x) = P(X ≤ x) = ∫₋∞ˣ f(t)dt; F'(x) = f(x) by FTC; F ranges from 0 to 1
Speeding up: v(t) and a(t) have the same sign; slowing down: they have opposite signs
The set of input values that make physical sense in the context; endpoints may produce absolute extrema
Vertical: where denominator = 0 (numerator ≠ 0); horizontal: limits at ±∞; oblique: when degree(num) = degree(den) + 1

quiz_questions.explanation: "Feasible domain" is defined as: The set of input values that make physical sense in the context; endpoints may produce absolute extrema. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Second derivative confirmation"?

options.text: The derivative of the natural log; note that d/dx[ln|x|] = 1/x for x ≠ 0
State the integral being evaluated, show the antiderivative, substitute limits, and simplify; credit for setup even if computation errors occur
If f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0: local minimum (concave up); f''(c) < 0: local maximum (concave down)
Find dy/dt given dx/dt (or vice versa) by differentiating an equation relating x and y with respect to time t

quiz_questions.explanation: "Second derivative confirmation" is defined as: If f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0: local minimum (concave up); f''(c) < 0: local maximum (concave down). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Absolute extrema on open intervals"?

options.text: Derived from the limit definition using sin(x+h) = sin x cos h + cos x sin h and special limits
f'''(x), f⁴(x), etc.; in physics: position → velocity → acceleration → jerk → snap → crackle → pop
If there is only one critical point and it's a local extremum, it is also the absolute extremum on the domain
Finding a specific antiderivative by using a condition like F(0) = 3 to determine the value of C

quiz_questions.explanation: "Absolute extrema on open intervals" is defined as: If there is only one critical point and it's a local extremum, it is also the absolute extremum on the domain. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Setting up the objective function"?

options.text: Identify what to maximize/minimize, express it as a function, and use constraints to reduce to one variable
f''(x) > 0; the graph curves upward like a cup; the slope is increasing
P = K: if the population is perturbed slightly above or below K, it returns to K
x-intercepts, y-intercept, local maxima and minima, inflection points, and behavior at asymptotes and endpoints

quiz_questions.explanation: "Setting up the objective function" is defined as: Identify what to maximize/minimize, express it as a function, and use constraints to reduce to one variable. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Use a constraint equation to express the objective function in terms of one variable before finding critical points"?

options.text: When does the particle change direction?
Conservative forces
Constraint optimization
Inflection point at P = K/2

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Constraint optimization".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The set of input values that make physical sense in the context; endpoints may produce absolute extrema"?

options.text: Acceleration
Domain restrictions
Feasible domain
d/dx[sin x] = cos x

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Feasible domain".

options.text: Lorenz curve
Total vs. net

Error in approximation
Notation

Concavity of F
Combined technique
When to simplify first

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Optimization and Analysis is accurate?

options.text: Limits and Continuity are the foundation of calculus.
A slope field (direction field) displays short line segments at grid points (x,y), each with slope equal to f(x,y) from dy/dx = f(x,y).
Antiderivatives and Indefinite Integrals introduce integration as the reverse of differentiation.
Optimization and Analysis combines first and second derivative analysis for comprehensive function behavior.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Optimization and Analysis as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: U-Substitution (Part 2): Advanced Techniques handles more complex substitution scenarios.

The optimization procedure: (1) model the situation with a function, (2) determine the feasible domain, (3) find critical points, (4) classify using first or second derivative test, (5) evaluate for absolute extrema including endpoints.
Arc Length and Surface Area uses integration to measure curve length and rotational surface area.

Key properties: ∫ₐᵇ [f ± g]dx = ∫ₐᵇ f dx ± ∫ₐᵇ g dx (linearity); ∫ₐᵇ cf dx = c∫ₐᵇ f dx (constant multiple); ∫ₐᵇ f dx + ∫ᵇᶜ f dx = ∫ₐᶜ f dx (additivity over intervals); ∫ₐᵃ f dx = 0; ∫ₐᵇ f dx = -∫ᵇₐ f dx (reversing limits).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Optimization and Analysis as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: These combined techniques are essential for related rates problems and advanced curve analysis.
Graphs also reveal asymptotic behavior and discontinuities.
Constraint optimization reduces multi-variable problems to single-variable: use the constraint to eliminate one variable before differentiating.
These derivatives are essential for modeling growth, decay, and natural phenomena.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Optimization and Analysis as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: For logarithms: d/dx[ln x] = 1/x and d/dx[logₐ x] = 1/(x ln a).
Optimization and Analysis combines first and second derivative analysis for comprehensive function behavior.
Mean Value Theorem: Applications and Consequences explores the theoretical power of MVT.
The chain rule 'peels off layers' of composition: differentiate the outer function (leaving inner unchanged), then multiply by the derivative of the inner.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Optimization and Analysis as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: Differential Equation Basics introduces equations involving derivatives.

The optimization procedure: (1) model the situation with a function, (2) determine the feasible domain, (3) find critical points, (4) classify using first or second derivative test, (5) evaluate for absolute extrema including endpoints.
Linear approximation uses the tangent line to estimate function values near a known point: f(x) ≈ f(a) + f'(a)(x-a).
The derivative of an inverse function is (f⁻¹)'(a) = 1/f'(f⁻¹(a)), derived from the chain rule applied to f(f⁻¹(x)) = x.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Optimization and Analysis as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: Minimizing material for a cylindrical can: given volume V = πr²h, minimize surface area A = 2πr² + 2πrh by expressing h in terms of r using the volume constraint
Disease spread: the SIR model's infection curve resembles logistic growth, with the inflection point marking peak new infections
Simple harmonic motion: position x(t) = A sin(ωt) gives velocity v(t) = Aω cos(ωt) and acceleration a(t) = -Aω² sin(ωt), showing oscillatory behavior
AC electrical circuits: voltage V(t) = V₀ sin(ωt) has derivative V'(t) = V₀ω cos(ωt), the rate of voltage change used in capacitor/inductor analysis

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Optimization and Analysis.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: Long-term drug concentration models: horizontal asymptotes represent the steady-state drug level that the body approaches with regular dosing
Maximizing the area of a rectangular field with a fixed perimeter of fencing (classic perimeter constraint → square is optimal)
Financial mathematics: differentiating revenue functions R(x) to find marginal revenue and determine the optimal quantity to maximize profit
Computer-aided design (CAD): finding normal vectors to complex curved surfaces defined implicitly requires combined differentiation techniques

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Optimization and Analysis.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Optimization and Analysis applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Modeling the trajectory of a projectile with air resistance: the implicit equation relating position components requires combined chain rule and implicit differentiation
Electrical engineering: integrating sinusoidal voltage V(t) = V₀sin(ωt) across a capacitor to find charge: Q = ∫V dt = -(V₀/ω)cos(ωt) + C
Minimizing shipping costs when warehouse location affects both transportation distance and delivery time—a practical business optimization problem
Submarine hull design: calculating pressure at operating depth determines hull thickness needed to prevent collapse

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Optimization and Analysis.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Optimization and Analysis?

options.text: Minimizing material for a cylindrical can: given volume V = πr²h, minimize surface area A = 2πr² + 2πrh by expressing h in terms of r using the volume constraint
Disease spread: the SIR model's infection curve resembles logistic growth, with the inflection point marking peak new infections
Computer-aided design (CAD): finding normal vectors to complex curved surfaces defined implicitly requires combined differentiation techniques
A ball thrown upward returns to its starting height: Rolle's Theorem guarantees it had zero velocity (was momentarily at rest) at some point during its flight

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Optimization and Analysis.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Optimization and Analysis in practice?

options.text: Maximizing the area of a rectangular field with a fixed perimeter of fencing (classic perimeter constraint → square is optimal)
Acceleration as the second derivative of position: a car speeding up has positive s''(t), slowing down has negative s''(t), and constant speed has s''(t) = 0
Bacterial growth: E. coli doubles every 20 minutes in ideal conditions; 1 bacterium becomes ~70 billion in 12 hours (2³⁶ ≈ 6.9 × 10¹⁰)
Long-term drug concentration models: horizontal asymptotes represent the steady-state drug level that the body approaches with regular dosing

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Optimization and Analysis.
